reviews.author: Horst Birthelmer
reviews.summary: Reviewer Horst Birthelmer questioned the necessity of the patch changes, citing it as a 'pretty big change' for a 'corner case', and asked about the advantage of the requested modifications from Miklos and Bernd.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, I got confused there, sorry.
Still, a pretty big change for a corner case. Don't you think?

What would be the advantage to the current situation with the requested 
changes from Miklos and Bernd, of course? So that we only do the 
wakeup_all() call only if we have initialized a new inode?

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert expressed concern that the patch's behavior, which updates attributes before unlocking an inode, may not be immediately apparent in all code paths and could lead to issues if not properly understood by future developers. He suggested a specific condition for truncating page cache and invalidating inode pages, and questioned the use of '!=' instead of '<'.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah, you want to update the attributes before unlock_new_inode()? The
risk I see is that I don't imediately see all code paths of
truncate_pagecache and invalidate_inode_pages2. Even if there is no
issue right, who would easily notice the fuse behavior in the future.
I kind of agree with that method if the condition would be

		if (oldsize > attr->size) {
			truncate_pagecache(inode, attr->size);

and I don't understand why it is '!='. I.e. for a new inode oldsize
would be 0 - the condition would never trigger and my concern would
never be true.

Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author addressed Horst's concern that fuse_change_attributes_i() takes the fi lock, not the inode lock, and responded by explaining that a small patch would be added to unlock the new inode after initialization.

Author responded to Horst's feedback about fuse_change_attributes_i() taking the fi lock instead of the inode lock, agreeing that this eliminates the race and is a cleaner solution.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author addressed a concern that fuse_change_attributes_i() takes the fi lock instead of the inode lock, and explained why it's safe not to take the inode lock in this function because truncate_pagecache() and invalidate_inode_pages2() are gated by conditions that will always be false for I_NEW inodes.

Author addressed a concern that the condition in fuse_change_attributes_i() could trigger an inode initialization race, explaining that fuse_iget() sets inode->i_size to attr->size before calling fuse_change_attributes_i(), making the condition impossible to trigger.
reviews.raw_body:
We never call into truncate_pagecache() or invalidate_inode_pages2()
from fuse_change_attributes_i():
 truncate_pagecache() is gated by oldsize != attr->size:
    for I_NEW inodes, fuse_init_inode() sets inode->i_size to
attr->size, which means "oldsize != attr->size" is alwyas going to be
false

  invalidate_inode_pages2() is gated by oldsize != attr->size and
"!timespec64_equal(&old_mtime, &new_mtime)" (where new_mtime  ses the
mtime values from attr):
    for I_NEW inodes, fuse_init_inode() calls inode_set_mtime(inode,
attr->mtime, attr->mtimensec);, which means
"!timespec64_equal(&old_mtime, &new_mtime)" is always going to be
false

---

For a new inode, oldsize is attr->size, not 0. The condition never triggers.

fuse_iget() calls fuse_init_inode() (which sets inode->i_size to
attr->size) before it calls fuse_change_attributes_i().

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the condition in truncate_pagecache() and invalidate_inode_pages2() never triggers for I_NEW inodes because fuse_init_inode() sets inode->i_size to attr->size, making oldsize != attr->size always false.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, I just see it, had missed that before. Your patch version sounds
good to me then.


Thanks,
Bernd

2026-03-26.patch_summary: This patch fixes a race condition in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module related to inode initialization. The issue arises when a new inode is created and its attributes are changed immediately after, causing the truncate_pagecache() and invalidate_inode_pages2() functions to be bypassed due to incorrect conditions. To fix this, the patch ensures that these functions are called even for new inodes by modifying the fuse_change_attributes_i() function.
